title: chewy argentinian oats empanadas
description: Fermented, chewy, festive argentinian dish featuring rolled oats, brine-wet, pescatarian-friendly. Perfect for breakfast or snack
instructions: Combine oats, yeast, and warm water in a large mixing bowl. Mix well until smooth, then let sit in a warm place for 1 hour or until slightly effervescent.
Add ancho chili powder, salt, black pepper, and olive oil to the bowl. Mix until combined.
Stir in caramelized onion, fresh oregano, and egg. Mix until a cohesive dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes on a lightly floured surface until it reaches a smooth, pliable consistency.
Divide the dough into 8-10 equal pieces. Roll each piece into a ball, then flatten slightly into a disk shape.
Place a tablespoon of filling in the center of each disk. Fold the dough in half to enclose the filling, then press edges together to seal the empanada.
Heat a large skillet over medium heat, then add 1-2 tablespoons of water to the pan. Cook the empanadas for 4-5 minutes on each side, or until they're golden brown and crispy.
Serve warm, garnished with additional fresh oregano if desired.
Caramelize onion in a skillet over low heat for 20-25 minutes or until dark golden brown and fragrant. Let cool before using in empanada filling.
For a flaky, crispy crust, preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C). Bake the empanadas for 10-12 minutes, or until golden brown and crispy.
